channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The Trump administration late Friday said they would exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from reciprocal tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot in Nashville
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, KREX 5

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs want jurors at trial questioned about sex, drugs and violence
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Sean “Diddy” Combs are urging a judge to let prospective jurors at the hip-hop mogul's upcoming sex trafficking trial be questioned about their views regarding sex, drugs and violence.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 major unreleased movies we'll (probably) never get to see
item.description: "Coyote vs. Acme" was recently rescued from obscurity after being shelved by Warner Bros. Discovery. But other films haven't been so lucky.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former LAPD officer pleads guilty to murder in DUI crash that killed family of 3
item.description: A former Los Angeles police officer was off-duty and speeding at 150 mph when he crashed into two vehicles on Sept. 26, 2017.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Side nonprofit Project HOOD rings New York Stock Exchange opening bell
item.description: Project HOOD provides educational resources and tutoring to thousands of students, helping them achieve academic success.
item.dc:creator: Micah Materre, WGN 9

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: U.S. measles cases topped 700 as of Friday, capping a week in which Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks, Texas grew by another 60 cases and a third measles-related death was made public. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Kohberger's evidence fight, Vallow's latest trial, Read's jury selection
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ says whereabouts of Maryland immigrant wrongly deported to El Salvador are unknown
item.description: The DOJ says the whereabouts of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland-based Salvadoran immigrant wrongfully deported to an El Salvador last month, are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department tasked with enforcing Trump's transgender sports ban in Maine
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine officials said Friday the state will not comply with a ban on transgender athletes in high school sports in the wake of a Trump administration finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing the students to participate. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: Six people were killed when a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander after learning she sent an email with a slight against the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Some Texas families impacted by SNAP cuts; food banks brace for direct hit
item.description: LEANDER, Texas (KXAN) — Chari Seeley happily looks at a list of items. She points to the cabbage and cheese. A few seconds later she shouts, "whipping cream!" It's not something she would usually get but she explained it's for a special occasion. "It's my birthday," she sang, laughing.
item.dc:creator: David Barer, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, ABC 4

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must facilitate return of deported Maryland man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must facilitate the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Brawl erupts between college baseball teams
item.description: A fight broke out Tuesday night during a game between the Spring Hill College and University of Mobile baseball teams.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House GOP adopts Trump budget blueprint after last-minute scramble
item.description: House Republicans on Thursday adopted the Senate’s framework that will be used to enact key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, getting the blueprint over the finish line after a last-minute scramble to win over conservatives who had spent days railing against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Johnson presses ahead with vote on Trump budget blueprint
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said he was pressing forward with a vote Thursday morning on the party's “one big, beautiful bill” full of President Trump’s legislative agenda, holding a joint appearance with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hope of finding survivors fades in aftermath of Dominican club roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Rescue crews in the Dominican Republic on Thursday dug through the remains of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed earlier this week, killing at least 184 people, but hope of finding survivors was slim.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents. The request was made Wednesday to the Postal Regulatory Commission, which must OK the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified as teenagers, according to media reports. The teens were discovered Jan.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5
